message:
fix(modal): propagate deferred focus target to base_focus when host regains focus after modal pop

When the terminal loses focus while a modal is active, then the modal
is popped, the base_focus restore target becomes stale — it still
points to whatever was focused before the modal was pushed. If
focus() is called while the host is blurred (which correctly defers
the target via pending_focus_on_host_gain), that deferred target was
ignored by ModalFocusCoordinator when the host regained focus because
it only checked self.base_focus.

Now, when the host regains focus with an empty modal stack,
ModalFocusCoordinator reads deferred_focus_target() before calling
set_host_focused(true), and if a deferred target exists, overwrites
base_focus with it. This ensures the most recent focus() call wins
even if it happened while the terminal was blurred.

Adds test:
- blurred_background_focus_change_after_last_modal_pop_overrides_stale_base_focus

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
